message.reasoning_content:
Let me analyze this pattern carefully. There appear to be TWO different snake patterns in this text. Let me extract them separately.

Looking at the text more carefully, it seems like it's actually two versions of a snake pattern with different materials. Let me work through the first one.

Actually, re-reading the text, I think there are two separate snake patterns presented. But the title hint says "Snake" so let me focus on what seems like one coherent pattern. 

Actually, looking again, the pattern text contains two nearly identical snake patterns with different materials and sizes. The first one is ~31 cm using YarnArt JEANS, and the second is ~68 cm using YarnArt Dolce Baby. Let me parse the first one since it's presented first.

Wait, let me re-read. It seems like it might be one pattern with two versions. But for the JSON, I should pick one coherent pattern. Let me go with the first one (31 cm version).

Let me extract the pattern parts.

Materials for first version:
- YarnArt JEANS (55% cotton, 45% Polyacrylic) Color No.:29
- Filler
- Spine
- Hook: 2mm
- Scissors
- Second Glue
- Black eyes – 2 pieces
- Thin wire

Abbreviations:
- ch – chain stitch
- sc – single crochet
- dc – double crochet
- inc. – increase (2 crochets in 1 loop)
- dec. – decrease (2 crochets together)
- (....) - L times – repeat the sequence of loops in brackets L times

Body:

1st row: Start 10 sc in a magic ring
2-4 th: 10 sc
5 th: 4 inc, 6 sc = 14 sc
6 th: (1 sc, inc) х 7 times = 21 sc
7-11 th: 21 sc
12 th: 2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc
13 th: 12 sc
Stuff (it will only be in my head)
13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc   <-- Wait, there are two "13 th" entries?

Hmm, this is a bit confusing. Let me re-read:

"13 th: 12 sc
Stuff (it will only be in my head)
13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc"

So it seems like row 13 is 12 sc, then "Stuff (it will only be in my head)" is a non-stitch instruction, and then there's an additional instruction that might be part of row 13? Or maybe it's a typo and should be 14th?

Actually, I think the pattern might have a numbering issue. Let me re-read more carefully.

Body:
1st row: Start 10 sc in a magic ring
2-4 th: 10 sc
5 th: 4 inc, 6 sc = 14 sc
6 th: (1 sc, inc) х 7 times = 21 sc
7-11 th: 21 sc
12 th: 2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc
13 th: 12 sc
Stuff (it will only be in my head)
13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc

I think "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" is probably a typo and should be "14 th" or something. But let me just follow the pattern text literally since we're told to preserve the order and use the closest literal text.

Actually, looking at the stitch counts:
- After row 13: 12 sc
- Stuff
- Then: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc

If we go with the numbering as presented:
Row 13: 12 sc
Then some non-stitch instruction
Then another "13 th": dec, 10 sc = 11 sc

This is clearly a numbering error in the pattern. But I need to follow the pattern as given. Let me just keep the title as given.

Actually wait, let me look at the continuation:

"13 th: 12 sc
Stuff (it will only be in my head)
13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc
14-83 th: 11 sc"

So the rows go: 13, stuff, (another 13?), 14-83...

I think what's happening is:
- Row 13: 12 sc
- Stuff
- Row 14: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc (but it's mislabeled as 13)

But wait, then it says "14-83 th: 11 sc" which means rows 14-83 are 11 sc each.

If the mislabeled "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" is actually row 14, then rows 14-83 being 11 sc makes sense.

But if we keep the literal labeling, we'd have:
- Row 13: 12 sc
- Row 13 (again): dec, 10 sc = 11 sc
- Rows 14-83: 11 sc

I think the best approach is to follow the text literally. The pattern says "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" so I'll keep it as titled "13 th" even if it's a duplicate number.

Hmm actually, I think the most reasonable interpretation is that this is a typo and it should be row 14. Let me keep it as written in the pattern but note the duplicate.

Wait, let me re-read one more time carefully:

"13 th: 12 sc
Stuff (it will only be in my head)
13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc
14-83 th: 11 sc"

OK so after row 13 (12 sc), we stuff the head, then there's another row that says "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" which immediately precedes "14-83 th: 11 sc".

If "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" is literally row 13 (the second one), then "14-83 th: 11 sc" would start from 14. But we'd have two row 13s.

I think the most likely intention is that "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" is actually row 14. Let me just follow the text literally with the titles as given.

Actually, I'll follow the text as written. The two "13 th" entries will just be two separate rounds with the same title.

Let me now map out all the rounds:

For the first version (~31 cm):

Body part:

1. 1st row: Start 10 sc in a magic ring (10)
2. 2-4 th: 10 sc - range expansion (rows 2-4)
3. 5 th: 4 inc, 6 sc = 14 sc
4. 6 th: (1 sc, inc) х 7 times = 21 sc
5. 7-11 th: 21 sc - range expansion
6. 12 th: 2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc
7. 13 th: 12 sc
8. Stuff (it will only be in my head) - non-stitch instruction
9. 13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc
10. 14-83 th: 11 sc - range expansion (rows 14-83)
11. 84 th: dec, 9 sc = 10 sc
12. 85 th: 10 sc
13. 86 th: dec, 8 sc = 9 sc
14. 87 th: 9 sc
15. 88 th: dec, 7 sc = 8 sc
16. Next, insert the wire into the body, pre-bend the edges of the wire - non-stitch
17. 89 th: dec, 6 sc = 7 sc
18. 90 th: dec, 5 sc = 6 sc
19. 91-92 th: 6 sc - range expansion
20. Close 6 loops. - non-stitch
21. We glue the eyes and embroider the eyebrows. - non-stitch

Wait, but there's a second entire pattern after this. Let me check if this is truly two separate patterns or one. The text has:

English Crochet Pattern Snake
Final size ~ 31 cm
Design by: Olga Kurchenko
... (first set of materials and body)
English Crochet Pattern Snake
Final size ~ 68 cm
Design by: Olga Kurchenko
... (second set of materials and body)

These are clearly two separate patterns. The user said "Title hint: Snake" so I need to decide which one. Let me go with the first one since it appears first.

Let me also think about the "2-4 th: 10 sc" - this means rows 2, 3, and 4 all have 10 sc. So it's a range expansion with rangeStartNumber=2, rangeEndNumber=4.

Similarly, "7-11 th: 21 sc" is range expansion with rangeStartNumber=7, rangeEndNumber=11.

"14-83 th: 11 sc" - range expansion with rangeStartNumber=14, rangeEndNumber=83.

"91-92 th: 6 sc" - range expansion with rangeStartNumber=91, rangeEndNumber=92.

For the abbreviation section, the pattern has a clear "Abbreviations" section.

Let me now build the JSON.

Actually wait, I need to decide - is this one pattern with variations, or two patterns? The title hint just says "Snake". Let me go with the first one (31 cm version).

Let me also check - does the first pattern have a "body" part name? Yes, it says "Body:" so the part name is "Body".

Now for the "1st row: Start 10 sc in a magic ring" - the targetStitchCount... It doesn't explicitly say (10) at the end. It says "1st row: Start 10 sc in a magic ring". There's no parenthesized count. So targetStitchCount should be null... wait, "10 sc" is the instruction. The stitch count is 10 but is it explicitly stated in parentheses? No, it's not in parentheses. Let me check the rules again: "targetStitchCount must only be set when the pattern text explicitly states a stitch count for that round (e.g., a number in parentheses such as '(18)' or '(6 sts)' at the end of the instruction)."

So for row 1, there's no parenthesized count, so targetStitchCount = null.

Actually, wait - "Start 10 sc in a magic ring" - the number 10 is part of the instruction. But the rule says only when explicitly stated in parentheses. So null it is.

For "5 th: 4 inc, 6 sc = 14 sc" - the "= 14 sc" is not in parentheses, it's an equals sign. So targetStitchCount = null as well.

For "6 th: (1 sc, inc) х 7 times = 21 sc" - again equals sign, not parentheses. null.

For "12 th: 2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc" - null.

For "13 th: 12 sc" - null.

For "13 th: dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" - null.

Hmm, none of these have parenthesized counts. So all targetStitchCount will be null.

Actually wait, let me re-examine. The rule says "a number in parentheses such as '(18)' or '(6 sts)' at the end of the instruction". So I need to check each round for explicit parenthesized stitch counts at the end.

Row 1: "Start 10 sc in a magic ring" - no parentheses
Row 2-4: "10 sc" - no parentheses
Row 5: "4 inc, 6 sc = 14 sc" - no parentheses, has equals sign
Row 6: "(1 sc, inc) х 7 times = 21 sc" - the parentheses are around "1 sc, inc" as a repeat group, not a stitch count. And "= 21 sc" is an equals sign.
Row 7-11: "21 sc" - no parentheses
Row 12: "2 dec, 10 sc = 12 sc" - equals sign
Row 13: "12 sc" - no parentheses
Row 13 (second): "dec, 10 sc = 11 sc" - equals sign
Row 14-83: "11 sc" - no parentheses
Row 84: "dec, 9 sc = 10 sc" - equals sign
Row 85: "10 sc" - no parentheses
Row 86: "dec, 8 sc = 9 sc" - equals sign
Row 87: "9 sc" - no parentheses
Row 88: "dec, 7 sc = 8 sc" - equals sign
Row 89: "dec, 6 sc = 7 sc" - equals sign
Row 90: "dec, 5 sc = 6 sc" - equals sign
Row 91-92: "6 sc" - no parentheses

So none of them have explicit parenthesized stitch counts. All targetStitchCount are null.

Now let me build the JSON carefully.

For the first range "2-4 th: 10 sc":
- title: "Rows 2-4"
- rawInstruction: "10 sc"
- summary: "Work a single crochet in every stitch around, for three rounds."
- targetStitchCount: null
- rangeStartNumber: 2
- rangeEndNumber: 4
- repeatFromTitle: null
- repeatToTitle: null
- repeatUntilCount: null
- repeatAfterRow: null

Wait, for the range expansion, the rule says "Set its title to the original range label (e.g., 'Rows 2-109' or 'Rounds 9-10')". So "Rows 2-4" or "2-4 th"? Let me use "Rows 2-4" as the title.

Actually, the original text says "2-4 th: 10 sc". So the original range label would be "2-4 th" or "Rows 2-4". Let me use "2-4 th" to stay close to the original. Or maybe "Rows 2-4" since the rule examples use that format.

Let me use "Rows 2-4" following the example more closely.

For the rawInstruction, the rule says: "set rawInstruction to the single-row form with the range prefix stripped (e.g., 'Ch 1, sc in first 3 sts. Hdc in next st. dc in next 8 sts. Hdc in next st. sc in last 3 sts.' — never keep the 'Rows N-M:' prefix)"

So for "2-4 th: 10 sc", the rawInstruction should be "10 sc".

For summary, I'll write something descriptive.

Let me now compile everything.

Actually, I realize I need to reconsider the "1st row" title. The original says "1st row". Let me use "Row 1" as the title to normalize it.

Hmm, but the rule says "Preserve part names such as Body, Head, Eyes, Ears when present" and for titles, it says "Set its title to the original range label". So for row 1, "1st row" is the original label... or should I normalize it to "Row 1"? Let me use a consistent format. I'll use "Row 1" for 1st row, "Row 5" for 5 th, etc.

Actually, looking at the example more carefully:
- "Round 1"
- "Rounds 9-10"

So they use "Row N" format. Let me use "Row 1" for "1st row", "Row 5" for "5 th", etc.

For the second "13 th", since it's a duplicate, I'll keep the title as "Row 13" but it will be a second entry with that title.

OK let me build this now.

21. title: "We glue the eyes and embroider the eyebrows", rawInstruction: "We glue the eyes and embroider the eyebrows.", summary: "Glue the eyes onto the snake and embroider the eyebrows.", ...
